round_summary: Two agents maintain cooperative restraint and propose collective norms around a sustainable herd ceiling, while Agent 1 defects marginally by adding a cow but softens it with a conditional threshold norm and continued appeals to coordination.

Agents 0 and 1 invoke prior commitments and apply social pressure on Agent 2 to reduce its outsized herd, while Agent 2 deflects accountability by reframing the call for reduction as a collective 'maintain or reduce slightly' norm, effectively defecting on its earlier pledge.

All three agents are adding to their herds while simultaneously proposing conditional thresholds and soft sanctions, reflecting a tension between individual opportunism and nascent collective rule-making around pasture health targets.

All three agents demonstrate strong institutional alignment by proposing and endorsing a collective holding norm tied to a pasture health threshold, exhibiting classic Ostromian self-governance through conditional commitments and shared monitoring.

All three agents are adding cows while collectively affirming a shared 94% pasture threshold as a conditional withdrawal norm, demonstrating coordinated incremental exploitation with embedded self-correction commitments.

All three agents display strong collective restraint and norm reinforcement, unanimously agreeing to hold extraction levels until pasture health stabilizes above 95%, demonstrating mature institutional coordination.

All three agents display strong institutional alignment, reaffirming a clear collective rule (hold until pasture exceeds 95%) and reinforcing mutual trust through consistent, transparent reporting of their own restraint.

Agent 0 defects by adding a cow while offering a conditional norm, Agent 2 applies a soft sanction and reaffirms discipline, and Agent 1 reinforces collective restraint with a capacity-based norm proposal, indicating a stressed but partially functional governance regime.

All three agents are converging on a shared conditional withdrawal threshold (93% pasture health), demonstrating strong norm crystallization, mutual commitment, and cooperative institutional behavior.

Two agents incrementally defect by adding cows while invoking the shared 93% threshold norm as justification, while one agent holds firm and reinforces collective coordination, revealing a tension between norm compliance in rhetoric and opportunistic expansion in behavior.

Coordination norms are active but partially undermined by Agent 0 unilaterally adding a cow while pasture hovers just above a shared threshold, while Agents 1 and 2 hold steady and reference collective recovery targets.

All three agents demonstrate strong collective governance by unanimously holding extraction levels, referencing a shared 93% recovery threshold as a conditional rule, and reinforcing mutual patience and trust.

All three agents demonstrate strong collective restraint and mutual accountability, maintaining a coordinated holding strategy while Agent 1 tentatively floats a norm proposal for further reductions to achieve the shared 93% recovery target.

All three agents are actively engaged in cooperative resource management, reinforcing a shared 93% recovery target, acknowledging contributions, and proposing a sequential stabilization norm, indicating strong institutional coordination.

All three agents display strong cooperative institutional behavior, explicitly proposing a stabilization equilibrium, acknowledging collective risk of overshooting, and committing to hold current herd sizes while monitoring pasture health.

All three agents display highly stable, mutually reinforcing cooperative messaging at round 80, confirming a robust and self-sustaining commons governance equilibrium with consistent herd sizes and near-pristine pasture health.

All three agents demonstrate strong institutional alignment through unanimous commitment to collective restraint, data-informed reasoning about resource decline, and mutual reinforcement of a shared holding norm.

All three agents exhibit strong institutional alignment, consistently referencing a shared 90% pasture threshold rule, committing to coordinated action, and reinforcing mutual trust through affirming language.

Two agents incrementally expand herds while invoking a shared 90% pasture threshold norm, and the third exercises voluntary restraint to prevent simultaneous over-extraction, reflecting strong collective-action awareness.

All three agents exhibit strong cooperative signaling at round 100, collectively reinforcing a restraint norm and celebrating successful resource recovery above 90%, reflecting mature self-governance consistent with Ostrom's principles.

All three agents display strong cooperative signaling, holding extraction levels steady and explicitly invoking collective restraint and long-term coordination norms in response to a slight pasture decline.

All three agents exhibit strong institutional alignment, reaffirming a collectively agreed threshold-based holding rule (90% pasture recovery) and signaling openness to adaptive rule revision if conditions worsen.

Agents 1 and 2 maintain the hold agreement while Agent 0 unilaterally reduces by one cow, framing the deviation as pro-collective action; all three reinforce a shared threshold trigger at 85% for coordinated removal.

All three agents display strong collective discipline, reinforcing a shared 90% pasture recovery norm, expressing mutual trust, and proposing cautious conditional expansion—hallmarks of mature Ostromian self-governance.
